pages.title: Introduction
pages.html:
Introduction

The purpose of this volume is to facilitate the use of cohomology by the non-expert \(\ell\)-adic. I hope that it will often allow him to avoid the use of the tufted presentations of SGA 4 and SGA 5. It also contains some new results.

The first presentation, written by J.F. Boutot, covers SGA 4. It gives the main results – with a minimum generality, often insufficient for applications – and an idea of their demonstration. For complete results, or detailed demonstrations, SGA 4 remains indispensable.

The "II" contains a complete demonstration of the formula of traces for the endomorphism of Frobenius. The demonstration is that given by Grothendieck in SGA 5, eluded from any unnecessary detail. This report should allow user to forget SGA 5, which can be considered as a series of very interesting digestion. Its existence will soon make it possible to publish SGA 5 as it stands. It is supplemented by the presentation "VI" which explains how the formula of traces allows the study of trigonometric sums, and gives examples.

The audience targeted by the other presentations is more limited, and their style is felt. The presentation "III" is a "modular" generalization of the Report, based on the study SGA 4 XVII 5.5 of symmetrical powers. The presentation "IV" defines this classes in various contexts, and gives the compatibility between intersections and cup-products. In "V" some known results are gathered, for which a reference is missing, and some compatibilities. The presentation "VII" is new. In particular, it gives, in cohomology without supports, theorems of finiteness similar to those known in cohomology with compact supports.

For more details on the presentations, I refer to their respective introduction.

Finally, I thank J.L. Verdier for allowing me to reproduce here his notes "VIII." They remain very useful, and useful: they become unobtainable.

In the internal references to this volume, the presentations are cited by an abbreviated title, which appears [ ] in the table of contents.

Bures-sur-Yvette, September 20, 1976

Pierre Deligne
